description: In shell, validate the argument parsing, IFS handling, and piping/redirection of untrusted input where external data crosses in.
body: In shell, defects cluster at argument parsing, IFS handling, and piping/redirection of untrusted input, where assumptions about types, ownership, or encoding stop holding. Use when a change crosses exit codes, stdout/stderr separation, and the documented argument interface or consumes data from outside the program. Do not assume the other side honors your contract, and avoid trusting input without checking it. Actions: identify the boundary, test both valid and malformed input, verify the error path, and record the assumptions you are relying on. Validation signals include tests for the failure path, a trace of rejected input, and a documented contract at exit codes, stdout/stderr separation, and the documented argument interface. Risk boundary: escalate when the boundary handles untrusted input, crosses a trust/security line, or feeds a system you cannot roll back.
